text.title: SSL/TLS Setup Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.description: Production HTTPS endpoint returns SSL_ERROR_BAD_CERT_DATE; customers locked out.
antipattern.reason: Manual renewal procedure forgotten; cert expired Saturday morning.
antipattern.note: Fix: Install cert-manager. Create Certificate resource. Automate via Ingress annotation. Backfill remaining manually-managed certs.

antipattern.description: SSL Labs scan grades B due to TLS 1.0 + RC4 enabled.
antipattern.reason: nginx config copied from a 2014 tutorial; never re-evaluated.
antipattern.note: Fix: Apply Mozilla Modern profile (ssl_protocols TLSv1.3; ssl_ciphers HIGH:!aNULL...). Run testssl.sh; iterate to A+.

antipattern.description: Penetration test flags missing HSTS; downgrade attack feasible.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add Strict-Transport-Security max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload. Submit to hstspreload.org.

antipattern.description: Wildcard cert used everywhere; one leak compromises all subdomains.
antipattern.note: Fix: Replace with per-host certs from Let's Encrypt (free). Wildcard reserved for cases with verified justification.

antipattern.description: TLS config regression goes unnoticed for weeks; flagged by external audit.
antipattern.reason: No scan in CI; nobody runs testssl.sh.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add testssl.sh to deploy pipeline. Fail on grade < A. Store the result as a change-record artifact.
